metadata.note: Iteration 2 scope only -- 3 new features + 3 updates. Iteration 1 implemented features not re-tracked.

gate.criteria: BRW-04 tests pass, registry CRUD operations verified, shared process_is_running utility extracted

All 4 features complete and tested. Launch registers instances, status lists them, attach connects with isolation, help resolves instance names. Cross-feature: launch -> status -> attach -> one-shot pipeline verified end-to-end.

CLI routing works for all command forms: operational commands, instance+method, bare method with default resolution. All iteration 2 tests pass. Full end-to-end: launch -> status -> attach -> one-shot -> help with instance names.

execution_strategy.phase_gates: All features in a phase must pass their tests and integration verification before the next phase begins. No regressions in prior phase tests.
execution_strategy.feature_selection: Follow the recommended order within each phase. Phase 2 order: BRW-05 first (simplest, validates registry), BRW-01 update (integrates registry with launch), CDP-04 (most complex, benefits from registry being exercised), CDP-03 (simplest update, last).
execution_strategy.blocker_management: If a feature is blocked by a spec gap, attempt to resolve from the spec's context. If blocked by an external system behavior, explore empirically. Escalate to user only for architectural decisions not covered by specs.

risk_assessment.risk: Most complex new feature -- browser-level WebSocket, Target.attachToTarget, event isolation, concurrent operation with one-shot
risk_assessment.mitigation: Implement after BRW-05 and BRW-01 so registry is well-exercised. Leverage experiment scripts from earlier in this session.

risk_assessment.risk: Three-way routing logic, backward compatibility, argument parsing changes
risk_assessment.mitigation: Implement last (Phase 3) so all dependencies are stable. Test every command form from the review agents' examples.
